refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to maintain the problem focus on safety without specifying how latency is measured or achieved, preserving solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise remote operation safety.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system to minimize delays
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication latency
Use an end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive control feedback loop
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system to minimize delays

potential_sources: Interaction between data routing and communication protocol
Dependency of latency monitoring on routing efficiency
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical data flow management, which can be implemented independently from specific communication protocols or compression methods, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not impose additional latency constraints
Predictive control is handled separately to avoid hidden coupling
Latency measurement accuracy is sufficient for adaptive control
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for latency monitoring and adaptive feedback to ensure continuous compliance with latency requirements and support dynamic network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data routing and scheduling logic for networked communication, managing prioritized data flows to minimize delays across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for monitoring and control data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands and scheduling inputs from higher-level control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends prioritized data packets over the network to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as routing and scheduling logically belong here; minimal diffusion since data routing is core functionality.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and sensing; routing logic would be diffused and less cohesive here.
Teleoperation involves remote control but routing/scheduling is a supporting function, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic; routing/scheduling would be poorly contained and highly diffused.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the prioritized data routing and scheduling system, as it directly manages network communication and data flow optimization.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules requiring careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If routing logic grows to include physical layer concerns, diffusion into Vehicle module may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system directly addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation. The DP logically targets minimizing delays, which is essential to meet the latency acceptance criterion (p95 ≤ 150 ms). Thus, the mapping from FR to DP is clear and appropriate.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules requiring careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If routing logic grows to include physical layer concerns, diffusion into Vehicle module may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP within a single module. The routing and scheduling logic is centralized here, minimizing diffusion risk. However, care must be taken to manage interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion as the system evolves.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) during teleoperation sessions using the NGHE Solution module's routing and scheduling capabilities.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and well-justified, with the module explicitly responsible for prioritized data routing and scheduling to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to manage coupling and prevent diffusion.
Monitor and control any expansion of routing logic into physical layer concerns to avoid diffusion into Vehicle module.
Include latency measurement and monitoring capabilities within the NGHE Solution module to support verification of the FR acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear, justified mappings and feasible verification. The DP and IM are well-contained with manageable diffusion risks, and the FR acceptance criteria are verifiable through the current implementation. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and monitoring rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and containment at this level is appropriate before proceeding further.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules, monitor routing logic expansion to avoid diffusion, and include latency measurement capabilities to support verification.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR of minimizing end-to-end latency for safe teleoperation.
The NGHE Solution module can feasibly implement and verify the prioritized routing and scheduling logic within the latency acceptance criteria.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if not carefully managed.
Risk of diffusion if routing logic expands into physical layer concerns within the Vehicle module.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and related modules.
Latency measurement data demonstrating p95 ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
Monitoring reports on routing logic scope and diffusion control.
